intro: Mollugophytum sperguloides, commonly known as creeping sand-spurrey, is a species of flowering plant in the family Molluginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Mollugophytum sperguloides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
